modules.fpm_special:
`timescale 1ns/1ps
module fpm_special(
    input         a_is_nan,
    input         a_is_inf,
    input         a_is_zero,
    input         b_is_nan,
    input         b_is_inf,
    input         b_is_zero,
    input         result_sign,
    output        is_special,
    output reg [31:0] special_result
);

    // Detect special cases
    wire is_nan = a_is_nan | b_is_nan;
    wire is_inf_times_zero = (a_is_inf & b_is_zero) | (b_is_inf & a_is_zero);
    wire is_inf = (a_is_inf | b_is_inf) & ~is_inf_times_zero;
    wire is_zero = (a_is_zero | b_is_zero) & ~is_nan & ~is_inf_times_zero;
    
    assign is_special = is_nan | is_inf_times_zero | is_inf | is_zero;
    
    always @(*) begin
        if (is_nan) begin
            special_result = 32'h7FC00000;  // Quiet NaN
        end
        else if (is_inf_times_zero) begin
            special_result = 32'h7FC00000;  // NaN (inf * 0 is undefined)
        end
        else if (is_inf) begin
            if (result_sign)
                special_result = 32'hFF800000;  // -Inf
            else
                special_result = 32'h7F800000;  // +Inf
        end
        else if (is_zero) begin
            if (result_sign)
                special_result = 32'h80000000;  // -Zero
            else
                special_result = 32'h00000000;  // +Zero
        end
        else begin
            special_result = 32'h00000000;
        end
    end

endmodule

fpm_multiply.approach: Implemented floating-point multiplication by computing result_sign as XOR of input signs, multiplying mantissas (with implicit leading 1 bit) as 24-bit values to produce a 48-bit product, and computing raw_exp as the sum of exponents minus the bias (127).

Fixed mantissa representation by properly left-shifting the 24-bit mantissa by 23 bits when forming the 48-bit scaled values, so the implicit leading 1 occupies bit 47 and the fractional part is correctly positioned in bits 46:0, ensuring the 96-bit product's upper 48 bits yield the correct normalized mantissa output.

Fixed mantissa scaling by left-shifting each 24-bit mantissa by 24 bits to create proper 48-bit fixed-point representations, then extracting the upper 48 bits of the 96-bit product to get the correctly normalized mantissa output.

fpm_round_pack.approach: Moved all variable declarations (`rounded_frac`, `rounded_exp`, `round_up`, `frac_with_implicit`) to module scope outside the `always @(*)` block to enable proper synthesis, and fixed rounding overflow handling to correctly incorporate the implicit leading 1 bit when detecting mantissa overflow.

Fixed rounding overflow detection by properly checking if adding 1 to the 23-bit fraction causes a carry (by using a 24-bit intermediate and checking bit 23), and correctly handling the case where rounding causes mantissa overflow requiring exponent increment.

Fixed the rounding logic to properly detect mantissa overflow by computing the rounded mantissa as a 24-bit value (with implicit leading 0 bit) and checking if bit 23 becomes set, which indicates the mantissa would overflow to 2.0 and requires exponent increment.
